provenance.source_corpus: Ecohydrological gradients and their restoration on the periphery of extracted peatlands

source_spans.text: Management actions on the periphery of extracted surfaces, including clear-cutting the edges of remnant peatlands, complete infilling of drainage canals, and surface reprofiling, improve hydrological conditions for peatland species establishment.
Managers can clear-cut trees along the edges of remnant peatlands in an attempt to reduce evapotranspiration and thereby increase the water table.
Managers can completely fill the peripheral drainage canals using peat from the adjacent remnant peatlands
Managers can reprofile the topography of peripheral remnant peatlands to make them more amenable to restoration
After these management actions, water tables increased to within approximately 5 cm of those along natural ecotones.

Management actions on the periphery of extracted surfaces, including clear-cutting the edges of remnant peatlands, complete infilling of drainage canals, and surface reprofiling, improve hydrological conditions for peatland species establishment.
water tables drop steeply within 8 m of blocked perimeter canals
Tree growth increased since the canals were dug and peatland extraction began
The drainage of peatlands reduces surface moisture conditions... which may directly reduce Sphagnum growth and presence
Drainage canals can lower the water table in peatlands over variable distances
